fields.description:
Tracks GitHub issue navapbc/oscer#767.

Part of the exclusion/exception determination work under epic #732 (Data Source Hierarchy). Depends on #766 and #761. Prerequisite for #768.

## Background

With exclusion signals represented as dates (#766), a signal that is not active during the certification month but whose dates fall within the prior lookback months indicates a mandatory exception rather than an exclusion. Today `ExclusionDeterminationService` records only exclusions, so this case produces no determination. This issue records the mandatory exception for it.

Because the service now determines both exclusions and exclusion-derived exceptions, it is renamed to `ExemptionDeterminationService`, exemption being the umbrella term for exclusions and exceptions.

## User Story

As a Medicaid program processing a certification, I want a former exclusion that lapsed into the lookback recorded as a mandatory exception, so that the member is correctly excepted rather than treated as having no applicable condition.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A date-based exclusion signal that is not active during the certification month, but whose dates fall within the lookback, records a mandatory exception.
- [ ] The determination still records an exclusion when the signal is active during the certification month (unchanged from #766).
- [ ] `ExclusionDeterminationService` is renamed to `ExemptionDeterminationService`, with references and the business-process step updated accordingly.
- [ ] The appropriate event is published for the exception outcome (`DeterminedExcepted` and its not-determined counterpart), preserving existing audit-log writes.

## Out-of-scope

- Evaluating exclusion and exception conditions in a single priority-ordered pass and selecting the most durable outcome. That is the follow-on issue, #768.
- Sourcing signals from external data sources (#755, #756).

## Dependencies & Risks

- Depends on #766 (exclusion signals as dates) and #761 (return the highest-priority exclusion).
- Reuses the month-level comparison from `ExceptionDeterminationService`; the shared helper can be extracted here or in #768, coordinating with #760.

## Links & Resources

- Data Source Hierarchy tech spec, Â§"Exceptions from former exclusions" (linked from epic #732)
- Related: #766, #761, #757, #765 (exception pattern), #760
fields.title: Record a mandatory exception when a former exclusion falls within the lookback
